issues_found: Minor timing discrepancy: Record states overnight rate was 'steady since September 2025 when it was cut from 2.50%', but source data shows rate changed from 2.50% in September 2025 to 2.25% in October 2025 — the cut occurred in October, not September

Summary and why_it_matters are thin: The record accurately reflects the data but lacks interpretation or analytical value beyond presenting numbers. For a data dashboard with no narrative content, the summary could be enhanced to better contextualize the significance
Source type 'data_dashboard' is appropriate but borderline on archival value threshold — it's purely tabular data without any commentary or analysis
human_review_reason: Issues are minor and factual. The record is mostly accurate with one small timing discrepancy in key_points. Source attribution is correct, event_type is reasonable, and key numbers are accurate. A human reviewer could quickly confirm the October 2025 rate cut timing and decide whether the summary adds sufficient value for archival purposes.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This Bank of Canada data dashboard provides the official monthly record of Canadian monetary policy settings and inflation trajectory. The overnight rate at 2.25% signals the current policy stance during an easing cycle from the 5.00% peak reached in early 2024, with inflation sitting at the lower end of the target band. This data is essential reference for tracking the evolution of Canadian monetary conditions.
corrected_fields.macro_context: Canada's overnight rate has declined from the 5.00% peak (Feb-Apr 2024) to the current 2.25% level as of February 2026. The rate was cut from 2.50% to 2.25% in October 2025 and has remained unchanged since. Total CPI at 1.8% is at the lower end of the 1-3% target band, while core measures cluster around 2.3-2.4%, suggesting the Bank of Canada has successfully anchored inflation near target. The Canadian Effective Exchange Rate (CEER) was 113.76 in February 2026.
